text.title: go-error-handling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-error-handling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using pointer equality: if err == apperror.ErrNotFound { ... }
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fails after fmt.Errorf("%w", err) wrapping, JSON deserialization, or struct copy. errors.As handles all cases.

failure-mode.description: Reading only c.Errors.Last() when handlers append multiple errors via c.Error().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Earlier errors are silently dropped. If the pattern requires multiple errors (batch validation), iterate c.Errors and aggregate into an errors[] array in the response.

failure-mode.description: Calling c.JSON(status, ...) inside the handler for error paths instead of c.Error(err).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bypasses the centralized handler; if multiple middlewares also write responses, the client sees a garbled body or Gin panics on double-write.

failure-mode.description: Returning raw err.Error() to clients: c.JSON(400, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leaks database DSNs, query text, internal hostnames. Always intercept in middleware and return a safe AppError.Message.

failure-mode.description: Conflating errors.Is (sentinel match) and errors.As (type assertion).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: errors.Is checks identity/equality down the chain; errors.As checks type. Using Is on a struct pointer that was reconstructed always returns false.

failure-mode.description: Using Wrap() as a generic wrapper on every error including user errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wrap always produces 500. User-facing errors (400, 404, 409) must use typed constructors that carry the correct status.
